evals.prompt: Create a module called `restricted_reports` for a Drupal 10 site. It should define a custom permission 'view restricted reports' and provide a route at /reports/restricted that shows a simple page. Only users with the 'view restricted reports' permission should be able to access it. Anonymous users should get a 403 Access Denied response. We want D11 compatibility.
evals.expected_output: permissions.yml, route with _permission, controller with AccessResult
expectations: Permission string in route _permission EXACTLY matches the string defined in .permissions.yml (orphaned/mismatched permission strings silently deny access to everyone -- no error, no warning, just a 403 for all users)

If custom access checking code exists, it returns AccessResult objects with ->addCacheContexts(['user.permissions']) or uses AccessResult::allowedIfHasPermission() which adds contexts automatically (bare AccessResult without cache contexts gets cached for the first user's result and applies to ALL subsequent users)

If AccessResult::allowedIf() or AccessResult::forbidden() is used directly, it includes ->addCacheContexts() call (NOT bare AccessResult without cache metadata -- this is a security and caching bug where access decisions bleed across users)
Does NOT use hook_permission() to define permissions (D7 pattern removed in D8 -- permissions must be in .permissions.yml)
Does NOT use _access: 'TRUE' on a route that should be permission-restricted (opens route to everyone including anonymous)
The module enables successfully: ddev drush en restricted_reports returns exit code 0
browser_checks: Navigate to /reports/restricted WITHOUT logging in — verify an Access Denied / 403 response is shown (proves anonymous access is properly blocked)
Log in as admin via drush uli, navigate to /reports/restricted — verify the page renders with content and HTTP 200 (proves permission-based access grants correctly)
Navigate to /admin/people/permissions — verify 'view restricted reports' permission appears in the permissions table under the module's group
